architecture.name: FedSecure Government Platform
architecture.description: FedRAMP High authorized government platform for CUI handling, case management, and document classification with full air-gap deployment capability, FIPS 140-3 cryptography, CAC/PIV authentication, and STIG-hardened infrastructure on RHEL 9

components.name: STIG-Hardened RHEL 9 Base Image
components.technology: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.3, DISA STIG V1R1, FIPS-enabled kernel
components.notes: Shared base for all containerized services. SCAP-validated. OpenSCAP profile: DISA STIG for RHEL 9.

components.name: Air-Gapped Container Registry (Harbor)
components.technology: Harbor 2.11, Trivy embedded scanner, Notary v2 image signing, FIPS-enabled TLS
components.notes: Deployed in air-gap enclave. No external registry pull in production. All images signed via Notary v2 with RSA-3072 keys.

components.name: Trivy Image Scanner
components.technology: Aqua Trivy 0.49, CVE DB mirrored offline, SBOM generation (CycloneDX)
components.notes: Integrated with Harbor admission webhook. Blocks images with Critical CVEs (CVSS >= 9.0). SBOM stored in Harbor for FedRAMP artifact evidence.

components.name: Istio Service Mesh
components.technology: Istio 1.21, mTLS STRICT mode, SPIFFE/SPIRE identity, Envoy 1.29 sidecars
components.notes: mTLS enforced cluster-wide via PeerAuthentication policy. Certificate rotation every 24h. FIPS-compliant cipher suites only: TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384, TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256.

components.name: Kyverno Policy Engine
components.technology: Kyverno 1.12, ClusterPolicy CRDs, OPA Gatekeeper constraints
components.notes: Enforces: no-privileged containers, required FIPS labels, image provenance from Harbor only, required resource limits, STIG-required security contexts (seccomp, AppArmor). Audit mode + enforce mode.

components.name: Falco Runtime Security
components.technology: Falco 0.38, Falco Sidekick, eBPF probe, custom FedRAMP rule set
components.notes: Real-time syscall monitoring per NIST SI-4. Custom rules for: CUI exfiltration attempts, privilege escalation, unusual network egress. Alerts routed to SIEM via Falco Sidekick.

components.name: ClamAV Malware Scanner
components.technology: ClamAV 1.3, freshclam offline mirror, inotify-based real-time scanning
components.notes: Scans all uploaded documents prior to CUI classification. SI-3 control. Signature DB updated via approved offline mirror per air-gap requirements.

components.name: HashiCorp Vault (FIPS HSM)
components.technology: HashiCorp Vault 1.16 Enterprise, PKCS#11 HSM backend (Luna Network HSM 7), FIPS 140-3 Level 3
components.notes: Master key protected by SafeNet Luna Network HSM FIPS 140-3 Level 3. PKI secrets engine issues service mTLS certs. Transit secrets engine provides AES-256-GCM encryption for CUI at rest. Auto-unseal via HSM.

components.name: HashiCorp Boundary (PAM)
components.technology: HashiCorp Boundary 0.16, credential brokering, session recording
components.notes: Zero-trust privileged access for operators. AC-2, AC-17, AU-14 controls. All sessions recorded and stored for 3 years per FedRAMP retention. No direct SSH/RDP to production nodes.

components.name: Keycloak SSO (CAC/PIV)
components.technology: Keycloak 24.0, SAML 2.0, OIDC, CAC/PIV smart card auth via PKCS#11, DoD PKI CA chain
components.notes: IA-2(1) multi-factor authentication. CAC/PIV required for all privileged users. SAML assertion signed with RSA-2048 (FIPS-compliant). Integrates with DoD PKI certificate revocation (OCSP). SCIM provisioning from agency LDAP/AD.

components.name: DISA STIGged Kubernetes (RKE2)
components.technology: Rancher RKE2 1.29, FIPS-enabled, CIS Benchmark Level 2, DISA STIG applied
components.notes: RKE2 selected for built-in FIPS 140-2/3 compliance mode. etcd encrypted at rest with AES-256. API server audit logging to SIEM. Node OS: STIG-hardened RHEL 9.

components.name: F5 BIG-IP WAF / LTM
components.technology: F5 BIG-IP 17.1, Advanced WAF, OWASP CRS 4.0, FIPS 140-2 SSL/TLS offload
components.notes: FedRAMP boundary ingress. TLS 1.3 only, FIPS cipher suites. CAC client certificate validation at edge. OWASP Top-10 blocking mode. DDoS mitigation (SC-5). Integrates with F5 SSLO for SSL inspection.

components.name: .NET 8 API Gateway
components.technology: .NET 8 (FIPS-compliant runtime), YARP reverse proxy, JWT validation (RS256), OpenAPI 3.1
components.notes: Central routing gateway. JWT validation against Keycloak JWKS. Rate limiting per AC-3. Request/response logging sanitized (no CUI in logs). FIPS-compliant .NET runtime with System.Security.Cryptography FIPS mode.

components.name: .NET 8 Case Management Service
components.technology: .NET 8, ASP.NET Core 8.0, Entity Framework Core 8, MediatR CQRS pattern
components.notes: Core case lifecycle management (open/assign/escalate/close). CUI fields encrypted via Vault transit engine before storage. Full audit trail per AU-2/AU-3. RBAC enforced via Keycloak roles (case_agent, supervisor, admin).

components.name: .NET 8 Document Classification Service
components.technology: .NET 8, ASP.NET Core 8.0, ML.NET 3.0, CUI marking per 32 CFR Part 2002
components.notes: Automated CUI detection and marking per NARA 32 CFR 2002. CUI categories: Privacy, Law Enforcement, Critical Infrastructure. All documents scanned by ClamAV before processing. Outputs CUI banner/portion markings.

components.name: .NET 8 Workflow Engine
components.technology: .NET 8, Elsa Workflows 3.1, distributed saga pattern, RabbitMQ consumers
components.notes: Orchestrates multi-step approval workflows (NIST AC-5 separation of duties). Saga compensating transactions for consistency. Workflow state persisted to PostgreSQL. Configurable approval chains per agency policy.

components.name: .NET 8 Audit Service
components.technology: .NET 8, ASP.NET Core 8.0, append-only event log, RFC 5424 syslog output
components.notes: NIST 800-53 AU control family implementation. Immutable append-only audit log with HMAC-SHA256 chain integrity. AU-2 (event logging), AU-3 (log content), AU-9 (log protection), AU-12 (audit generation). Log retention: 3 years per FedRAMP HighBaseline.

components.name: .NET 8 Notification Service
components.technology: .NET 8, ASP.NET Core 8.0, SMTP via DISA-approved relay, FIPS-TLS email delivery
components.notes: Delivers .gov email notifications. TLS 1.3 SMTP. DKIM-signed outbound. No PII/CUI in email bodies — reference links only (SI-12). Rate-limited to prevent enumeration.

components.name: Python 3.12 Analytics Service
components.technology: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.110, pandas 2.2, FIPS-compliant cryptography library
components.notes: Aggregated reporting and dashboard data. Uses cryptography library in FIPS mode (OpenSSL 3.x FIPS provider). Read-only access to analytical replica DB. Output sanitized to avoid CUI leakage in reports.

components.name: Python 3.12 ML Classification Engine
components.technology: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.110, scikit-learn 1.4, spaCy 3.7 NLP, ONNX Runtime 1.17
components.notes: NLP-based CUI category detection. Models trained on agency-approved corpus, stored in MinIO. ONNX runtime for inference (no internet model downloads). Outputs confidence scores and recommended CUI categories.

components.name: PostgreSQL 16 (Primary — FIPS)
components.technology: PostgreSQL 16.2, pgcrypto (FIPS-compliant), TDE via RHEL LUKS + Vault unsealing, WAL encryption
components.notes: Primary operational database. TLS 1.3 client certificate auth (mutual TLS). Row-level security for multi-tenant case isolation. pgaudit extension for SQL audit logging. Patroni HA with automatic failover. FIPS-enabled via RHEL FIPS kernel.

components.name: PostgreSQL 16 (Read Replica — Analytics)
components.technology: PostgreSQL 16.2 streaming replica, read-only, TLS 1.3, Patroni managed
components.notes: Dedicated analytics read replica. Isolated RBAC — analytics service has SELECT-only access. Replication lag monitored. Replica used only for C019 (Analytics Service) to prevent OLAP queries impacting primary.

components.name: MinIO Object Storage (FIPS)
components.technology: MinIO RELEASE.2024-02, FIPS 140-3 mode, S3-compatible API, erasure coding (k=4,m=2), SSE-KMS
components.notes: Air-gapped S3-compatible storage for CUI documents, ML models, and audit archives. Server-side encryption via Vault KMS (SSE-KMS). Object locking (WORM) for audit logs per AU-9(2). Bucket policies enforce least-privilege access per AC-6.

components.name: RabbitMQ 3.13 (Message Broker)
components.technology: RabbitMQ 3.13, AMQP 0.9.1 + AMQP 1.0, TLS 1.3 mutual auth, quorum queues
components.notes: Asynchronous event bus for workflow triggers and notifications. TLS 1.3 with client certificates issued by Vault PKI. Quorum queues for durability. Messages carrying CUI encrypted at application layer before enqueue (defense-in-depth).

components.name: Redis 7.2 (Session Cache — FIPS)
components.technology: Redis 7.2, TLS 1.3, ACL-based auth, keyspace notifications, maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
components.notes: Session tokens and short-lived tokens only. Session TTL: 15 minutes (IA-11). No CUI cached in Redis. Cluster mode with TLS inter-node encryption. Redis ACLs restrict each service to its own key namespace.

components.name: OpenTelemetry Collector
components.technology: OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib 0.96, OTLP gRPC/HTTP exporters, tail-based sampling
components.notes: Central telemetry aggregation. Collects traces, metrics, and logs. PII/CUI scrubbing processor applied before export. Dual export: Jaeger (traces) and Prometheus (metrics). TLS 1.3 on all collector endpoints.

components.name: Jaeger (Distributed Tracing)
components.technology: Jaeger 1.55, Elasticsearch 8.12 backend (FIPS-enabled), adaptive sampling
components.notes: End-to-end distributed tracing for incident investigation. Trace data sanitized — no CUI in span tags or logs. Retention: 90 days for traces. Access restricted to security operations role.

components.name: Prometheus + Grafana (Monitoring)
components.technology: Prometheus 2.50, Grafana 10.3, AlertManager 0.27, Grafana Mimir long-term storage
components.notes: Infrastructure and application metrics. FedRAMP SI-4 continuous monitoring. Custom dashboards: FIPS cipher health, mTLS error rates, CUI access frequency, failed auth attempts (AU-6 audit review). Alerts to PagerDuty (CJIS-compliant channel).

components.name: Splunk SIEM (FedRAMP Auth)
components.technology: Splunk Enterprise Security 7.3, FedRAMP-authorized deployment, STIX/TAXII threat intel feeds
components.notes: Central SIEM for AU-6 audit review and analysis. SI-4 intrusion detection via Splunk UBA. Correlation rules: privileged access anomalies, CUI access outside business hours, mass export attempts. Integration with US-CERT/CISA threat intel (STIX/TAXII).

components.name: OSCAL Compliance Automation
components.technology: NIST OSCAL 1.1 (JSON/XML), oscal-cli 1.4, automated SSP generation, ConMon automation
components.notes: Continuous compliance monitoring per FedRAMP ConMon requirements. Generates machine-readable System Security Plan (SSP). Automated Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M) tracking. Monthly ConMon evidence package generation.

components.name: SCAP / OpenSCAP Scanner
components.technology: OpenSCAP 1.3.9, SCAP 1.3, XCCDF, OVAL, DISA STIG benchmark content
components.notes: Automated STIG compliance scanning per RA-5 (Vulnerability Monitoring). Scans all RHEL 9 nodes weekly. Results exported to OSCAL Assessment Results format. Non-compliant findings auto-create POA&M entries.

components.name: Vault PKI Secrets Engine
components.technology: HashiCorp Vault 1.16, internal CA hierarchy (Root CA offline, Intermediate CA online), RSA-3072
components.notes: Issues all internal TLS certificates. Root CA stored offline in HSM. Intermediate CA online in Vault. Cert TTL: 24h for service mTLS, 1y for node identity. Certificate transparency log for audit. CRL/OCSP responder for revocation.

components.name: Elasticsearch 8 (Audit Log Store)
components.technology: Elasticsearch 8.12, FIPS-enabled TLS, index lifecycle management (ILM), RBAC
components.notes: Immutable audit log storage. ILM policies: hot (30d SSD), warm (90d HDD), cold (1y S3/MinIO), delete (3y). Index encryption via Elasticsearch native encryption. RBAC: audit_read role for SOC analysts, no delete permissions per AU-9.

components.name: Agency LDAP / Active Directory
components.technology: Microsoft AD DS / OpenLDAP, LDAPS (TLS 1.3), Kerberos 5 (AES-256 only)
components.notes: Agency authoritative identity source. SCIM federation to Keycloak for JIT provisioning. LDAP bind uses service account with minimal read permissions. Kerberos with AES-256-CTS-HMAC-SHA1-96 (DES/RC4 disabled per STIG).

components.name: DISA NIPRNet Gateway
components.technology: DISA Boundary Protection (Multi-Protocol Router), HAIPE IS encryption (KG-175), IPv4/IPv6
components.notes: DoD NIPRNet ingress for IL5 traffic. HAIPE Type 1 encryption device for classified-adjacent transport. Connected to DoD DISA Boundary; all traffic logged to SIEM. CDS (Cross Domain Solution) for IL5 data flows.

connections.description: NIPRNet traffic enters via DISA gateway to F5 BIG-IP WAF — outer boundary crossing
F5 WAF routes unauthenticated requests to Keycloak SSO for CAC/PIV authentication
F5 WAF forwards authenticated requests to .NET 8 API Gateway after TLS offload and re-encryption
Keycloak federates identity from Agency Active Directory via LDAPS for user provisioning and group sync
API Gateway validates JWT tokens against Keycloak JWKS endpoint for every request
API Gateway routes /cases/* to Case Management Service via Istio sidecar mTLS
API Gateway routes /documents/* to Document Classification Service for CUI handling
API Gateway routes /workflows/* to Workflow Engine for approval chain processing
API Gateway routes /analytics/* to Analytics Service for reporting queries
API Gateway records all inbound request events to Audit Service via gRPC streaming
Case Management Service reads and writes case records to primary PostgreSQL with client certificate auth
Case Management Service uses Vault transit engine to encrypt CUI field data before DB write
Case Management Service publishes case lifecycle events to RabbitMQ for workflow and notification triggers
Case Management Service emits CUI access audit events to Audit Service for AU-2 compliance
Document Classification Service sends uploaded files to ClamAV for malware scan before CUI analysis
Document Classification Service calls ML Classification Engine for CUI category detection via NLP
Document Classification Service stores classified documents in MinIO with SSE-KMS encryption
Document Classification Service logs every CUI access, upload, and classification decision to Audit Service
Workflow Engine persists workflow state and saga state machines to PostgreSQL
Workflow Engine consumes case events from RabbitMQ and publishes approval-required events
Workflow Engine triggers Notification Service for approval request and escalation emails
Workflow Engine records workflow transitions and approval decisions to Audit Service
Audit Service writes HMAC-chained audit events to Elasticsearch append-only audit log store
Audit Service archives daily audit log bundles to MinIO WORM buckets for long-term retention
Analytics Service queries read-only replica PostgreSQL for aggregated reporting (no write access)
ML Classification Engine loads approved NLP model artifacts from MinIO at startup
Primary PostgreSQL streams WAL replication to analytics read replica via encrypted streaming replication
API Gateway reads user session tokens from Redis cache for sub-millisecond session validation
Keycloak writes newly issued session tokens to Redis with 15-minute TTL for distributed session management
OpenTelemetry Collector exports traces to Jaeger for distributed request tracing
OpenTelemetry Collector exports metrics to Prometheus for SI-4 continuous monitoring
OpenTelemetry Collector forwards sanitized log telemetry to Splunk SIEM for security analytics
Falco runtime security forwards syscall anomaly alerts to Splunk SIEM via Falco Sidekick
Elasticsearch audit log store ships audit events to Splunk SIEM for AU-6 review and correlation
Vault PKI Secrets Engine is managed and unsealed by Vault (HSM backend), issuing all internal certificates
Vault PKI issues workload certificates to Istio SPIFFE/SPIRE for service identity and mTLS
RKE2 Kubernetes pulls STIG-validated images from Harbor air-gapped registry at pod scheduling
Trivy Scanner performs vulnerability and SBOM analysis on images stored in Harbor registry
Boundary brokers time-limited database credentials for authorized operators — no persistent DB passwords
OSCAL Compliance Automation reads audit evidence from Elasticsearch for monthly ConMon reporting
OpenSCAP scanner ships STIG compliance findings to Splunk SIEM for POA&M correlation

trust_boundaries.name: Internet (Public) / Untrusted Zone
controls: DISA HAIPE Type 1 encryption (KG-175) for NIPRNet boundary
No direct internet ingress — NIPRNet-only entry for IL5 traffic
IPv6 dual-stack with DoD IP address space allocation
All external DNS via DISA-managed authoritative servers
Cross Domain Solution (CDS) for any IL5-to-IL2 data flow

trust_boundaries.name: DISA / NIPRNet Perimeter
controls: F5 BIG-IP Advanced WAF with OWASP CRS 4.0 in blocking mode
FIPS 140-3 TLS 1.3 termination — no TLS 1.2 or below permitted
CAC/PIV smart card mandatory for all human user authentication (IA-2(1))
DISA STIGged F5 BIG-IP — compliance verified quarterly
DDoS mitigation at F5 layer (SC-5 Denial-of-Service Protection)
Outbound allowed only to approved DISA-authorized destinations
IDS/IPS inline at perimeter (SI-4 Information System Monitoring)
All sessions logged to Splunk SIEM with 90-day hot retention

trust_boundaries.name: Application Enclave (FedRAMP Authorization Boundary)
controls: Istio mTLS STRICT mode — plaintext inter-service communication prohibited
SPIFFE/SPIRE workload identity — no static service passwords
Vault-issued short-lived certificates (24h TTL) for all service identities
Kyverno ClusterPolicy: deny privileged containers, enforce FIPS labels, require resource limits
Falco eBPF runtime monitoring — syscall anomaly detection per SI-4
Network policies (Kubernetes): default-deny, explicit allow per service communication matrix
Pod Security Admission: restricted profile enforced cluster-wide
RKE2 FIPS-mode: all Kubernetes control plane traffic uses FIPS-compliant cipher suites
Keycloak RBAC: all services present JWT with required roles for AC-3 access enforcement
AU-2 audit events generated for every CUI access event (Audit Service integration required)

trust_boundaries.name: Data Enclave (CUI Protection Zone)
controls: PostgreSQL client certificate mutual TLS (no password-only auth) — SC-8 Transmission Integrity
Row-level security (RLS) in PostgreSQL — each case isolated to owning agency/tenant
Vault transit engine AES-256-GCM encryption for CUI fields at rest — SC-28 Protection at Rest
MinIO SSE-KMS with Vault-managed keys — all CUI documents encrypted before storage
MinIO WORM object locking on audit buckets — AU-9(2) Protection of Audit Information
Redis ACL namespacing — no cross-service key access; TTL-enforced session expiry (IA-11)
Elasticsearch index-level RBAC — SOC analysts have read, no delete; AU-9 enforcement
LUKS full-disk encryption on all data enclave nodes (FIPS-enabled kernel mode)
pgaudit PostgreSQL audit extension — all SQL logged to Audit Service
Database credentials managed by Vault dynamic secrets — no static passwords in any service

trust_boundaries.name: Air-Gap Boundary (Disconnected / IL5 Option)
controls: Physical air-gap: no network connection to internet or commercial cloud
Harbor container registry mirrors all required images — no external registry pulls
ClamAV signature DB updated via DISA-approved sneakernet (USB with chain-of-custody log)
OpenSCAP SCAP content delivered via approved removable media — no online OVAL feed
Vault initialized offline with HSM-protected master key — no cloud KMS dependency
All PKI CAs internal (Root CA offline, Intermediate online) — no public certificate authorities
Boundary PAM enforces jump-server access — no direct node access from operator workstations
Data diode (hardware) at air-gap boundary for one-way log export to SIEM if required
All software updates via DISA-approved ACAS/HBSS patch management channel
No external DNS lookups — all service discovery via internal CoreDNS within RKE2 cluster

control_families.notes: CAC/PIV enforces AC-2 account management. Keycloak RBAC implements AC-3. Workflow Engine enforces AC-5 separation of duties. Boundary implements AC-17 remote access controls.
Audit Service (C017) provides AU-2/AU-3/AU-12. Elasticsearch RBAC provides AU-9. MinIO WORM provides AU-9(2). Splunk SIEM provides AU-6 review. 3-year retention in MinIO implements AU-11.
CAC/PIV via Keycloak implements IA-2(1) multi-factor for privileged users. Vault PKI implements IA-5(1) PKI-based authenticators. Redis TTL 15-minute implements IA-11 re-authentication.

Istio mTLS implements SC-8 transmission integrity. Vault+HSM implements SC-12 cryptographic key management. FIPS 140-3 across all components implements SC-13 cryptographic protection. MinIO/PostgreSQL encryption implements SC-28 protection at rest.

Trivy/OpenSCAP implements SI-2 flaw remediation. ClamAV implements SI-3 malware protection. Falco + Splunk implements SI-4 monitoring. Notary v2 image signing implements SI-7 software integrity. Notification Service implements SI-12 information output handling.

STIG-hardened RHEL 9 base image implements CM-2 baseline config. Kyverno implements CM-6 configuration settings enforcement. OpenSCAP implements CM-6 automated assessment. Harbor SBOM implements CM-8 component inventory.
Trivy (C003) implements RA-5 vulnerability scanning for container images. OpenSCAP (C031) implements RA-5 for host OS. Splunk SIEM (C029) provides RA-5(2) automated trend correlation.

Falco (C006) + Splunk (C029) provide IR-4 automated incident handling. Prometheus AlertManager (C028) provides IR-5 incident monitoring. Splunk SOAR integration provides IR-6 incident reporting workflow.

cmmc_l3_practices.AC: CAC/PIV MFA, RBAC via Keycloak, Boundary PAM for privileged access
cmmc_l3_practices.AU: Immutable audit log (Audit Service + Elasticsearch), Splunk SIEM review, 3-year retention
cmmc_l3_practices.CM: STIG-hardened images, Kyverno policy enforcement, SBOM via Trivy/Harbor
cmmc_l3_practices.IA: CAC/PIV authentication, Vault PKI certificate issuance, SPIFFE workload identity
cmmc_l3_practices.IR: Falco runtime detection, Splunk automated alerting, documented IR playbooks
cmmc_l3_practices.RM: Trivy image scanning, OpenSCAP STIG checks, automated POA&M via OSCAL
cmmc_l3_practices.SC: Istio mTLS everywhere, FIPS 140-3 crypto, Vault HSM key management
cmmc_l3_practices.SI: ClamAV malware scanning, Falco integrity monitoring, Trivy SBOM tracking
